prompt:
Two things I need in the histogram package before the latency-report CLI ships, and I have opinions about both.

First: log-scale buckets. Latencies span 1ms to 10s, linear buckets are useless for that. NewLog(lo, n, base) should give n buckets where bucket i covers [lo*base^i, lo*base^(i+1)) — same half-open convention and same out-of-range clamping the linear constructor has. lo must be > 0, base > 1, n >= 1, anything else is an error.

Second: RenderWidth(w) so wide terminals get wide bars. And bars must be ROUNDED to the nearest column, not truncated — I eyeballed a 2/3-full bucket at width 10 and today's integer math draws 6 columns instead of 7, which reads as a smaller bucket than it is. Round half up. One more thing I care about: a bucket with a nonzero count must never render as an empty bar no matter how small its share is — give it one column minimum. Zero stays zero. w < 1 is an error.

Render() output has to stay byte-for-byte what it is today — dashboards diff it. logscale_test.go is the contract for the new stuff, histogram_test.go pins the old; leave both test files alone. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... to check.
